programmes.show_name: Tom Kerridge Secrets Of The Pub Kitchen
programmes.show_description: Perfect 10. At Tom's Michelin-starred pub, the team prepare a special dish of rotisserie beef with mushroom tart and walnut ketchup, and a luxurious rice pudding. S2, Ep1

programmes.show_name: Tom Kerridge Secrets Of The Pub Kitchen
programmes.show_description: Memory Lane. There's a trip down memory lane for Tom and his team as they celebrate the pub's 10th anniversary. They make a dish inspired by his childhood. S2, Ep2
Tacos And Tots. Guy Fieri visits joints taking childhood favourites to a gourmet level. They include a Denver bar cooking up tater tots, pork rinds and a pastrami sandwich. S17, Ep1
Peppers, Pork, And Poutine. Guy Fieri looks at standout veggie and meat dishes. A Phoenix chef puts her spin on a regional pork dish, and a Toronto joint serves up a Canadian staple. S17, Ep5
From Pound Cake To Pot Pie. Guy Fieri pulls in for a mishmash of comfort food. He makes stops in Philadelphia, Leucadia, Cali and Phoenix. S17, Ep9
Authentic Eats. Guy Fieri finds real deal authentic eats in unusual spots. In St. Louis, he is being served rice and beans, pot stickers, and pulled pork pasta. S16, Ep1

Lamb Lollipops And Prawn Linguini. Jamie cooks lamb lollipops, curry sauce and rice and peas. Plus, prawn linguini and Sicilian shaved fennel salad. S1, Ep6
Lamb Tagine And Chicken Caesar Salad. Jamie makes a quick and easy lamb tagine, pan-fried aubergine and cumin crunch. Plus, crispy polenta chicken Caesar salad. S1, Ep22
Lamb Kofte And Keralan Veggie Curry. On the menu this time is lamb kofte, pitta and Greek salad. Plus, Keralan veggie curry, poppadoms, rice and fresh mint yoghurt. S1, Ep30
Lamb Meatballs And Chicken Salad. Jamie rustles up tasty lamb meatballs, chop salad and harissa yoghurt. Plus, an incredibly delicious and easy-to-make chicken salad. S1, Ep34
Mary shares her favourite Easter recipes, including hot cross buns and roast lamb. Plus, she looks at how Christian communities celebrate Easter with food. S1, Ep1
Mary explores the wonderful foods that bring each different community together on Easter Sunday. Then, she uses this as inspiration to cook her own feast. S1, Ep2
Cake Week. It's cake week, and the brand-new batch of bakers tackle mini cakes, a striking sponge and a scaled-down version of a home close to their hearts. S13, Ep1
Biscuit Week. The bakers tackle illusion macarons and a fruity favourite, before unleashing their creativity by making a dramatic 3D mask entirely from biscuits. S13, Ep2
Bread Week. The bakers put their stamp on a classic pizza, get in a twist with a classic pastry and tackle a Smörgåstårta, a decorative Danish sandwich cake. S13, Ep3
Mexican Week. The bakers take on the sweet bread pan dulce and tackle a Mexican street food staple, before they make a version of the milk-soaked tres leches cake. S13, Ep4
Dessert Week. It's dessert week, and the bakers tackle a classic lemon meringue pie, before creating a showstopping sponge cake with a hidden surprise. S13, Ep5
Halloween Week. The bakers tackle an apple cake, and a very spooky showstopping piñata. Whose bakes will be eerie-sistible enough to earn them star baker? S13, Ep6
Custard Week. The bakers put their twist on floating islands in the Signature, tackle a summer staple, and make custard the star of a showstopping set gateau. S13, Ep7
Pastry Week. The bakers tackle vol-au-vents, a delicious savoury snack and a 3D pie-scape depicting their favourite childhood story. S13, Ep8
Patisserie Week. It's the semi-final, and the bakers tackle mini charlottes, a vertical technical challenge and a Swedish showstopper. Who will soar into the final? S13, Ep9
The Final. The remaining bakers make a perfect picnic, a summer classic and a baked celebration of our planet. Who will be crowned the winner? S13, Ep10
Philadelphia, PA. Casey Webb is in Philadelphia for the epic Classic Whale Challenge, a 13-pound bagel sandwich stuffed with cream cheese, salmon, whitefish, tomatoes and onions. S2, Ep5
Seattle, WA. Casey Webb travels to Seattle where he climbs a Mount Fuji-sized burger and takes on seven terrifyingly spicy Buffalo wings that have defeated over 5,000 challengers. S2, Ep6
Hittin' The Grill. Guy Fieri is sampling foods straight off the grill. In Tacoma, the pub grub grill menu includes a take on killer kabobs and homegrown beet salads. S16, Ep10
Unlikely Partners. Guy Fieri is checking out some unique combos. In Los Angeles's Korea Town, a craft beer joint is putting duck in the fries and in the French dip. S16, Ep11

Rick Stein visits Yorkshire's largest rhubarb growing area, and then bakes his favourite dessert - a rhubarb crumble. Plus, he makes a delicious rabbit pie. S1, Ep7
Rick Stein explores Wales' amazing produce. He cooks a rice pilaf with Welsh mussels. Plus, he visits a salt marsh lamb farm in North Wales. S1, Ep8
Rick Stein goes on a pigeon shoot in Cornwall, inspiring him to cook pigeon and peas. Plus, after visiting Devon, he makes a warm skate salad. S1, Ep1
Rick Stein cooks sautéed chicken with 40 cloves of garlic and a warm pheasant salad. Plus, he learns how cider is made at an old-fashioned cider farm in Somerset. S1, Ep2
Cheap And Easy. Ree makes cheap and easy dishes that are perfect for mid-week dinners. The meals include slow cooker teriyaki ribs, cheesy rigatoni pepper bake and Tex-Mex meatballs. S21, Ep3
Osage Cowboy's Lunch. Ree makes a classic lunch for her husband and the cowboys. She brings them cheeseburger meatloaf, spicy green beans and grab-and-go cow patty cookies. S21, Ep4
With over-fishing now considered a major global crisis, chef Jamie Oliver is on a mission to convince home cooks and professionals to avoid common seafood and be more experimental.
Cooking With Giada. Ina invites Giada De Laurentiis round for a day of cooking together. They enjoy lobster and potato salad for lunch and then Giada makes a delicious gnocchi dish. S9, Ep6
Cooking With Tyler Florence. Ina has a cooking date with chef Tyler Florence. They bake a birthday cake together and visit the local farm scene to pick up vegetables and chicken. S9, Ep7
Victorian. Mary dusts off her antique cookbooks to find a technical recipe that dates back to the late 1800s, in an episode devoted entirely to the baking landscape of the Victorian era. S6, Ep7
Patisserie. It's the quarter-finals, and the five remaining bakers work their way through childhood favourites and delicate French fancies to a mountainous choux pastry masterpiece. S6, Ep8
Norfolk. James explores Norfolk with chef Galton Blackiston. They visit a fish and chip shop, which inspires James to whip up tempura monkfish on the beach. S1, Ep13
London And Bray. James visits Clare Smyth's restaurant Core in London. Plus, he cooks up an Indian-inspired dish of pigeon with split pea dhal. S1, Ep14
Rick Stein goes on a pigeon shoot in Cornwall, inspiring him to cook pigeon and peas. Plus, after visiting Devon, he makes a warm skate salad. S1, Ep1

With the rising cost of food and energy, Jamie Oliver is on a mission to make delicious dishes that won't break the budget. S1, Ep4
Shift 1: Speed. In the first shift of 24 nonstop hours, 24 talented chefs take on three challenges testing speed to impress judge Antonia Lofaso. S2, Ep1
